title: Membership
content:
# Membership

We will welcome new members for the Fall 2026 curling session. [Check out our events page to find other opportunities to try curling this season](/index.php/events/event-listings).

Registration for Winter 2026 leagues is closed. Our next league registration period will begin in August, 2026.

#### **2025-2026 Membership Rates**

Our membership and league dues structure has changed for the 2025-26 season. [Check out our dues calculator](https://public.tccnc.club/dues/) to estimate your dues and see your membership benefits. 

#### **Joining a League**

Thinking about curling in a league? You're in the right place. When you register for a league, you become a member of Triangle Curling Club. Beyond league participation, your membership provides several additional benefits, such as unlimited practice time and sparing. [See all membership benefits](/index.php/leagues/membership/2-uncategorised/514-membership-benefits).

#### **New curlers**

**Currently, new member registration is limited to anyone who has already taken a Learn to Curl at Triangle Curling Club, experienced curlers from other clubs, and juniors joining our Junior Recreational program.** 

We welcome new curlers at Triangle Curling! There are two easy paths to get started:

**Path 1.** Our Saturday Instructional Program is an eight-week program that combines instruction and game play. From the fundamentals to strategy and etiquette, this is an excellent way to get started in curling. This program is priced the same as a regular league ([more on fees here](https://public.tccnc.club/dues/)).

**Path 2.** If you'd rather jump into a league, you can do that too! Take a single Learn-to-Curl class and follow it up with an Introduction to League Play, and you will be fit to join any of our regular curling leagues.

**Combine them!** If you're extra eager to get the most out of curling, you can sign up for the Saturday Instructional Program and a regular league at the same time.

If you would like to build a solid foundation before you join a league, Path #1 is the way to go. If you want to jump right in and learn as you go, Path #2 is for you. We have many curlers who have been successful with both paths, so it is a personal preference which you may prefer. Regardless of the path you choose, you will become a member of Triangle Curling Club.

#### **How to register**

Registration opens twice per year to align with the beginning of league sessions:

1. Mid-August for our fall leagues
2. Mid-December for our winter leagues:

\*Note: In Winter Olympic years, we may have a 3rd league session in the spring with registration opening in March.

* When registration is open, you will see a link at the top of this page. Click it and fill out the form to register.
* After you submit your registration, you will receive a PayPal invoice to pay for your leagues. Please note that it may not be immediate but will be sent at least 1-2 weeks prior to the start of leagues. **Your registration is not complete until this invoice is paid.**
* After payment is received, you will receive follow-up emails regarding your online account, and you will be added to the rosters for leagues you have selected.
* You will receive additional email communications from your league coordinators before the first day of leagues.
* Want to be notified when registration opens? [Sign up here](/index.php/leagues/membership/2-uncategorised/500-join-our-mailing-list).

#### **Junior curlers in leagues**

Triangle Curling welcomes juniors ages 5 and up in our Junior Recreational and Junior Advanced Commitment programs. See [Youth Curling](/index.php/curling/youthcurling) for more information on those programs.

All of our leagues are considered adult leagues. However, junior members ages 12 to 17 may join these leagues after obtaining permission from one of our juniors coordinators. While all Triangle Curling members are expected to follow the [Code of Conduct](https://links.tccnc.club/conduct), parents should be aware that accommodations for juniors cannot be guaranteed in leagues, including members' use of language or alcoholic beverages. Junior members that have demonstrated knowledge of the game, its rules, etiquette, and basic proficiency of gameplay may contact a juniors coordinator at [contact email] to receive permission for league play.

#### **Additional links you may find helpful**

* [Leagues](/index.php/leagues/league-info) (dates, formats, and more)
* [Juniors programs](/index.php/curling/youthcurling)
* [Membership FAQ](/index.php/leagues/membership-and-leagues-faq)
* [Membership benefits](/index.php/leagues/membership/2-uncategorised/514-membership-benefits)

#### **Questions?** 

Many questions about membership and league registration are answered in our [Membership and Leagues FAQ](/index.php/leagues/membership-and-leagues-faq). If you can't find the answer there, please contact [contact email].

---
